INCIDENTTITLE: Theft attempt of Broken Anode piece
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 3rd August 2024, SIC Nandkishore Patel received an intel regarding suspicious of lifting some material from anode unloading area by ESM Bhupal Singh. Knowing this, SIC Nandkishore Patel checked the bag of ESM Bhupal Singh at the time of exit of B shift at about 2210 hrs. Bhupal Singh was deployed at unloading area in B shift which starts from 1400 hrs and ends at 2200 hrs when relieved by reliver of night shift. Bhupal Singh picked broken anode pieces and hidden in a piece of cloth and picked. Further, he hidden the anode copper piece inside his bag, and kept it near the security main gate area. He was caught red-handed during security check at gate by Mr. Nandkishore Patel, and the material was recovered from his possession. He was interrogated in the Security cabin by Head Security Ananda and SIC Nandkishore, Peregrine Incharge Jitendra to which he confessed and provided his statement to the SIC where he accepted his mistake

INCIDENTTITLE: Gathering attempt at ZSD site at 28 Jun 2024
INCIDENTDETAILS: Today, at approximately 0935 hrs, Heera Lal arrived at ZSD to seek updates, accompanied by a letter signed by at least 50-60 locals, demanding immediate solutions to the gas discharge and employment issues (letter copy shared earlier). Heera Lal sought a meeting with the CSR head but only met with the NGO coordinator. He departed around 1015 hrs. The meeting concluded peacefully at ZSD. Credible sources have corroborated that a covert meeting took place between ZSD representatives and Heera Lal to negotiate and address his repeated attempts to instigate large-scale protests at ZSD. During this meeting, Heera Lal was assured that an coordinator would be specifically assigned to Gowla village to address ongoing issues. Furthermore, he was informed that ZSD had taken immediate measures to resolve the excessive gas discharge in nearby areas. Sources confirmed that he was assured of employment for himself and 5-6 key personnel recommended by various leaders. Unconfirmed sources have also indicated that union leaders have agreed to employ some locals, including Heera Lal, from Gowla to sustain locals support, Notably taking situational advantage for upcoming union & panchayat election.

INCIDENTTITLE: IR at CLZS(CHANDERIYA) site at 2024-08-09 05:30
INCIDENTDETAILS:
While checking the area outside the LMV parking (Near Sanwariya Hotel), S/G Mohd. Babu overheard some individuals that they are planning to block all gates. He noticed that they 
were around 40-50 in nos. who got off from a passenger Bus No- RJ09PA-6494 which was 
parked at some distance from the outer gate. Anticipating some anti-social elements and 
forceful entry, he immediately reported the matter to the Security Control Room and 
simultaneously closed the gate preventing their entry into the parking area. Their activities 
were monitored through CCTV Cameras & simultaneously QRT was rushed to the gate areas.
As they came to the Outer main Gate, the gate was immediately closed. Additional manpower 
from non-essential posts were also moved to gates. They then sat in protest in-front of gates 
blocking the movement of personnel & vehicles at CLZS outer gate, LMV Parking, Material
Gate, New SLF Gate and Parking Plaza gate. It was learnt that they were the relatives of Late 
Sarwan Singh Ranawat (Ex- Employee of M/S SS & Co.) and were staging a protest for 
claiming additional compensation from the BP. The group was being led by Surendra Singh 
Ranawat S/O Late Sarwan Singh Ranawat resident of Bhilwara,Rajasthan.

INCIDENTTITLE: Material at SILVASSA site at 2025-01-28 12:08
INCIDENTDETAILS:
On 28/01/2025, at about 2215hrs, Civil Security Guard Sunil Maurya of Peregrine who was posted at Unloading Area came to gate and asked Security SIC Yeeshu Chauhan that Peregrine vehicle will not be having adequate place to sit, and he want to go in Vedanta shift bus. SIC allowed him to go but he immediately went out bypassing the security checks. By this action of Civil Security Guard Sunil Maurya, SIC got suspicion and stopped him and checked his bag in which copper scrap material was found. It was about 5.4 Kgs.
Immediately the matter was informed to CSO G Mallesham and CSO in turn he has apprised the same to BU Director. CSO has come to the spot and summoned Peregrine Branch Manager Mr Radheshyam Gupta and Peregrine Field Officer Mr.Shafik Ali into security office. Both Peregrine officials reported to the security office, in their presence individual has been questioned about the incident. CSO has conducted thorough enquiry in which it was found that Sunil Maurya took the copper material from Loading and Unloading point. Thereafter he intended to sell this material to scrap collectors. Further he stated that he has committed this act for the first time and no other personnel involved and no co-accomplice. Civil Security Guard Sunil Maurya admitted his act and apologized for his misconduct. After the investigation CSO has asked Peregrine Branch Manager Mr. Radheshyam Gupta to takeaway Sunil Mourya and produce him the next day if required.

officer Ms. Parvathi Nair rushed to the spot and separated them and pacified.

INCIDENTTITLE: Foiled theft attempt on vendor vehicle near Piparia main gate
INCIDENTDETAILS:
On 05/02/2025, copper ingot loaded truck KA567479 was parked in front of Piparia main gate. At about 2240hrs. Main Gate SG Ashwin observed suspicious activity of a person near the truck. He immediately alerted the truck driver Rajkumar, and security supervisor ExSM Biren Singh. On this, Biren Singh along with ExSM Balwinder Singh reached the spot and Balwinder Singh climbed over the truck and found a miscreant in the truck, trying to cut open the tarpaulin. 
 
Observing Balwinder Singh, the miscreant attacked him with a glass bottle jumped off the truck and tried to run away. Security Team captured the individual. SIC Yeeshu Chauhan informed matter to all the concerned and in turn CSO G. Mallesham apprised to Head External Affairs and IBU director and local police. Police have arrived by 2325hrs and took the miscreant to Silvassa Police Station. 
Further, a formal police complaint has been given by CSO G. Mallesham to the Station In charge officer Silvassa Police Station. Pressing for appropriate action against the miscreants who are repeatedly attacking on inbound trucks at Piparia which is tarnishing the image of the company and the community.

INCIDENTTITLE: Person Related at VAB site at 29 Aug 2024, 11:48 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
On 29th August 2024 at around 08:40 hrs. at PID 2 main gate, during G shift movement, Mr. Sunil Kumar Yadav of M/s Monomark Engineering inappropriately touched a female VL staff while he was in queue for Punching In / gate entry. VL staff immediately informed the matter to team security.
Immediately Mr. Sunil was taken into custody for the investigation and the matter informed to the Security Command Centre, duty officer and CSO, meanwhile the matter was also informed to CHRO-VAB.
The duty officer, along with CHRO rushed to the incident location and investigated the case. 
In investigation the following points were revealed.
•	Today there was an extra foot fall in PID-2 unit due to shut down activity in Sinter Plant. 
•	Mr. Sunil is working in PID-1 under M/s Monomark (earlier M/s VAAMAN), he is working in current role since last 1.5 years.
•	Today Mr. Sunil was assigned some work in the shutdown activity in Sinter Plant.
•	There was sudden rain at around 0835hrs which created a crowd at the access terminal.  
•	Mr. Sunil accepted his mistake to touch the VL staff however he said that it was unintentional and happened due to sudden rain and rush at access control terminal.
•	Mr. Sunil apologized to the VL staff for his act.
•	Individual has been blacklisted.

INCIDENTTITLE: Organized theft in collusion with insider at VAL J site at 09 Aug 2024, 5:15 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On date 9/8/2024 M/s Unique Power (OH vendor of U- 5 under NGSL) BP loaded NRGP material in two pick-ups registration no (CG 11 AJ 0188) and (CG 12 BL 6977) as the vehicles were not loaded in the presence of auditor and security, which was against the SOP. Therefore, both the vehicles were thoroughly examined as per NRGP documents and 51 additional items were found in the vehicle. The extra material has been recovered and both vehicles have been loaded as per NRGP paper quantity and released. Mr. M Suresh, site in-charge of M/s Unique Power, wrote a letter that he had contacted the auditor before loading and the auditor had told him to first take the loaded vehicle and then come to him and he was not aware of the security presence, after Mr. Sudipta asked him to get his signature from security and all the material belongs to their company and they have valid entry papers and bills will submit shortly.

INCIDENTTITLE: SOP- LSR Violation at VAL J site at 04 Mar 2025, 5:15 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
"At around 1715hrs RGP material (RGP Plant2 20241104-11129) containing a Hexagonal Top Gasket valued at approximately INR 99,000/- of cast house came at the SEZ gate for Exit reason was for repair purposes. Upon inspection, it was found that this material, which has remained unused since 2010, does not fit the machine. Initially, a cast house employee stated that he had checked and cleared the quality of the material when it arrived. He also mentioned that this was project material. This came after 15 years later. The RGP documentation states 'repairing of damaged items,' which is misleading as the material is new and unused. Currently, the material is on hold for further investigation.
"

INCIDENTTITLE: Unauthorized entry/ Intrusion/Trespassing at TUTICORIN site at 2024-07-26 00:30
INCIDENTDETAILS:
On the intervening night of 25/26th July 2024 at 0030hrs a message was received from security guard of OS parking duty post that a person was roaming with a Billhook (Long sword) outside parking to Tamria#1 town ship gate. Message flashed to shift I/c SO Ramamurthy and Security HoD G Mallesham. In turn as per HoD's instructions the matter was informed to out Post Police of the area. Duty SO has accompanied the police to the spot and apprehended the person. Further on investigation by the police it is learnt that the person belongs to TV Puram and his name is Mr. Ramar and motive for venturing in the location was not revealed. Thereafter, police taken him to their custody along with the sword. As per the latest information on the matter the person is mentally disturbed and after counselling police let him off without registering any FIR/entry.
Note:-  Photos uploaded but not reflecting in the ISIMS portal report

Action taken: 
1.	The Security Team swiftly acted upon the incident and alerted the police and assisted them in apprehending the person.

Measures implemented to prevent such occurrences

1.	Security patrolling has been increased in and around Tamira#1 and OS parking area.  
2.	Night guards at lone post are briefed and ensured personal safety measures.
3.	Communication equipment such as mobile phones, whistles and torch lights were ensured at all lone guard duty posts.

The fire control room, QRT and shift in-charge were immediately informed. Around 12:26 hrs Successfully controlled, there is no report of anyone being injured.

INCIDENTTITLE: Others at RDC site at 04 Oct 2024, 9:00 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On October 4, 2024, at HZAPL, around 18:50 hrs, a loaded zinc cathode vehicle made contact with an electrical wire near Sindesar Kalan village, resulting in the wire breaking and hanging down. The villagers stopped the vehicle for 1 hour and 20 minutes. Around 21:00 hrs, four villagers from Sindesar Kalan arrived at the HZAPL gate by car and inquired about the exit of the zinc cathode vehicle from the plant. All four villagers were intoxicated and misbehaved with the security supervisor. Some of them forcefully entered the plant, seized the key to the cathode vehicle, and left.

INCIDENTTITLE: Theft attempt at BW near ETP at ZSD
INCIDENTDETAILS: At around 0725 hrs on 23rd Oct'24, an attempt of theft was reported to the security team, with a staircase found placed against the boundary wall ETP area. Team found that an attempt to stole the 03 Valve was made by group of unidentified individual who crossed the Bw using staircase. Immediate action was taken as all security teams were mobilized for an internal sweep to assess missing items and gather physical evidence. Teams during check outside BW recovered crucial evidence including slipper, cap, mobile phone, and stolen material. Also, the ingress and egress routes were mapped. Simultaneously police team was aligned for external support and police complaint was registered. As of now site team is engaging with law enforcement to ensure apprehension of perpetrators.

INCIDENTTITLE: Theft/Pilferage at VAL J site at 27 Nov 2024, 12:00 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: At approximately 12AM. A diesel tanker belonging to Krishna Build Con Pvt. Ltd. (Vehicle No.:  CG13W9656) was allowed entry with 500 liters of diesel recorded as the material entry. When the vehicle was sent for weighting, its tare weight was recorded as 2.3 MT, and its gross weight was recorded as 2.52 MT. This resulted in a net weight of 0.22 MT. However, upon calculation, a discrepancy of 236 liters was found, which is significantly higher than the normal permissible difference of 100 liters. As a result, the vehicle has been held at the Banjari Gate for further investigation.After investigation it was found that wrongly it was mentioned 500ltr where as in main approval 300ltr was mentioned

Input gathered from the Fire and Safety team reveals that during a routine maneuver, the driver of the fire tender, named *Mr Sarvan engaged with M/s Bonace (Vehicle No. HR63 B 9908)* inadvertently collided with the vehicle *(Vehicle No. RJ27 CF 0846 Maruti Suzuki white car)* of the *Shanti lal Dangi* a local villger from Tus Dangi village near Dabok area. INCIDENTTITLE: Access Related Violations at BALCO site at 2024-06-04 07:35
INCIDENTDETAILS:
On June 4, 2024, at approximately 0735 hrs, Mr. Arvind Kumar Yadav (UID 1645811) and Mr. Shyam Sundar (UID 1645812), business partners of M/s Larsen & Toubro Limited (Subcontractor M/s Kamla Enterprises), were apprehended for proxy punching while attempting to exit the premises. SG Sudama Das Manikpuri, stationed at the Parsabhata Pedestrian gate, caught Mr. Arvind Kumar Yadav in the act and promptly notified the authorities. Mr. Shyam Sundar attempted to exit during the C shift. Instead of retrieving his gate pass, which was left in the room, Mr. Arvind Kumar Yadav , provided his gate pass to  Mr. Shyam Sundar for exiting the plant. The Security Supervisor, Mr. R B Chandrakar, conducted a subsequent investigation which revealed that  Mr. Arvind Kumar Yadav facilitated this unauthorized exit.The gate passes of both individuals were confiscated.
Statements from Mr. Arvind Kumar Yadav (UID 1645811) and Mr. Shyam Sundar (UID 1645812) were recorded.

INCIDENTTITLE: Incidents against personnel at CCP site at 05 Jun 2024, 8:30 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
Bijay Kumar Raut (EMP ID - 710913) currently Working as Asst. security officer at Facor Plant.

Wednesday 05/06/2024 at 0830 Hours he was standing Opposite side of Sarathi hotel in Randia market, Bhadrak - 756135,  he had gone to buy fish from Randia Market and while standing there infront of Fish shop he was approached by Subham Kumar Das S/o Sarat Kumar das (EMP ID - CCP0004930) who used to work at Facor plant Under contractor M/s PAPL previously, but recently entry of Subham Kumar Das had been restricted to the plant premises - Subham was angry due to the same reason and asked to Bijay "Tune Mera entry kyuu band Kiya?'' - Bijay has complaint that he could smell from his breath that he was under the influence of alcohol.

INCIDENTTITLE: Theft/Pilferage at VGCB site at 03 Jun 2024, 11:45 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
Our proactive measures included intensifying patrols, enhancing surveillance in vulnerable areas, and collaborating closely with local law enforcement agencies. By deploying these strategies, we were able to deter the would-be thieves and protect our resources successfully. I am pleased to report that as a result of our swift response and the dedicated efforts of our security personnel, no incidents occurred. However, it's imperative that we remain vigilant and maintain a heightened level of alertness to forestall any future attempts.
Root Cause: Low Illumination at SY-4
Action Taken: Handed over to Harbour Police

INCIDENTTITLE: Offsite Incidents at VGCB site at 02 May 2024, 9:33 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
"On around 02.05.2024, 21:33 hrs, Incident occurred, during the night shift.
We received a call from our intelligence person alerting us to suspicious activity in the TT4 area. The caller reported that four individuals were attempting to steal coal in that area. Upon receiving this information, immediate action was taken.
Our Quick Response Team (QRT) was promptly alerted and mobilized to the scene. However, upon arrival, the individuals fled the area, evading apprehension by our team.
While the attempted theft was thwarted this time, it highlights the ongoing need for vigilance and proactive security measures in our operations. We will continue to monitor the situation closely and take necessary precautions to prevent such incidents in the future."
Root Cause: "There is no security deployment and it is completely open area. The complete area is not under any Camera."
Action taken: Increased more time to patrolling in that area.

REASON STATED BEHIND STRIKE BY THEM WAS THAT DURING FINAL MEETING OF STRIKE THAT TOOK PLACE IN JULY 2024, IT WAS DECIDED THAT THE OTS PAYMENT WOULD BE CLEARED BY NOVEMBER 2024. THEY WAITED TILL JANUARY, BUT AS IT WAS NOT CLEARED THEY DECIDED TO TAKE ACTION ON THAT RESULTING TO STRIKE.
IN ORDER TO SORT OUT THE MATTER , VEDANTA OFFICER, SIDDHARTH BEHERA ,MANOJ BEHERA CAME &  DISCUSSED THE MATTER AND AGREED THAT THE OTS WILL BE CLEARED BY 15TH JANUARY 2025. THE STRIKE WAS OVER AND  TRUCK MOVEMENT STARTED.

INCIDENTTITLE: Material at BALCO site at 07 Jan 2025, 5:00 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 07.01.2025, at approximately 1700 hrs., BCPP Security Supervisor Mr. Prabhjit Singh (Surendra Engineering) and Civ. SG Deepak Keshari (G4S) observed suspicious activities involving business partner workers from "M/s Lift & Shift" near the Cooling Tower area. Based on their observations, the security team approached the workers and conducted an on-site interrogation. During the investigation, the workers admitted to attempting to steal copper cable bundles weight of approx 4 kg., value of Rs. 1600, from the scrap yard area. They further confessed to throwing the stolen material outside the boundary wall near the Cooling Tower area. The stolen material was recovered from outside the boundary wall in the Cooling Tower vicinity. Statements from all four workers involved have been recorded for reference. Additionally, a statement from Mr. Chakrash Prasad, I/C of M/s Lift & Shift (UID-1563002), has also been documented. The matter has been escalated to the HR department for further necessary action.

INCIDENTTITLE: Select at RDC site at 27 Aug 2024, 9:14 PM

INCIDENTTITLE: Select at BALCO site at 08 Jan 2025, 7:30 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 08.01.2025, at approxiamately 1930 hrs., during regular frisking at the Project Gate worker movement gate, SS Chandan Singh caught approximately 1 Kg. of copper wire , valued of Rs. 400/- inside the bag of a business partner worker from M/s ABB India Limited (subcontractor: Shri Ram Enterprises) who was attempting to steal the material. The incident was immediately reported to Plant 1 Security Shift S/I Jitendra Gupta, who promptly arrived at the scene. A thorough investigation by the Project Zone I/C confirmed the involvement of Mr. Manohar Singh Kanwar of M/s ABB India Limited (subcontractor: Shri Ram Enterprises) in this pilferage attempt. Mr. Manohar Singh Kanwar was attempting to take the copper wires outside the plant by hiding them inside his bag. He had stolen this material from the GAP area, where he was working as a helper. During the interrogation Mr. Manohar Singh Kanwar admitted to the act of pilferage. Following the interrogation, his statement was recorded, and he was sensitized about the issue before being sent out of the plant premises. His gate pass was confiscated, and the matter has been escalated to the concerned HR for further action.

INCIDENTTITLE: Access Related Violations at BALCO site at 2025-02-06 22:26
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 06.02.2025, at approximately 2226 hours, during routine CCTV surveillance, CSOC observed four unidentified individuals entering the plant premises near the BLC backside post. Immediately relayed the information to the nearest deployed security guard at the BLC post, the Quick Response Team (QRT), and the Plant 1 shift in-charge. Upon their prompt arrival, the security team spotted the intruders, who, upon noticing the response, fled the scene without taking any items. A thorough search of the area was subsequently conducted, leading to the recovery of approximately 18 centering iron pipes, weighing 170 kg and valued at Rs. 3,400.The recovered material has been securely placed in a safe location,

INCIDENTTITLE: Unsafe Act at VAB site at 10 Jan 2025, 12:30 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: security team was having some reliable information regarding contraband item use and Prohibited activity in a operational room in front of MCD canteen. Accordingly, an ambush was deployed to intercept the activity. During this operation, a company workman, Mr. Vishwan Paryekar, was caught along with some prohibited materials, three bottles were recovered from that room, one of which had a strong smell of alcohol and there was another bottle that resembled an artificially crafted Hookah and third one of water. Initially Mr. Vishwan was not cooperating for the investigation and immediately he picked that crafted bottle and thrown it into Furness and claimed that this is his personal bottle, so he is disposing off despite of asking not to do so as that bottle was prime evidence in the case. Later on we connected with night shift IC Mr. Jayesh Parab than with his intervention he agreed to further course of action. He was taken to OHC for drug and Alcohol test, both the test was found negative.

INCIDENTTITLE: Assault at BALCO site at 2024-12-27 19:30
INCIDENTDETAILS:
On 27.12.2024, at approximately 19:30 hours, our security guard, Santosh Patley, who was on duty in the ICU ward of Balco Hospital, encountered an unfortunate incident involving a group of outsiders.
A group of 05-10 individuals, who were reportedly relatives of a patient (relatives of Dalmia)admitted to the ICU, started moving in and out of the ICU ward in a large group. The security guard informed them that such movement was not permitted in the ICU ward and requested them to visit one by one, considering the sensitive nature of the area.
However, the individuals disregarded his instructions and began assaulting him. As a result, the guard sustained injuries, including bleeding on his face. Following the assault, the group forcibly took the injured security guard to the police station in their Vehicle. A Police compliant and an FIR has been filed against the individual for further course of action.

All gates were reinforced, and shift movements were redirected to the Alumina gate. By 1800 hours, after discussions, the strike ended, gate operations resumed, and road movement restarted. The protesters were moved to a tent, which was later removed.

INCIDENTTITLE: Collision
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At around 0140 hrs QRT 1 informed Security Ops Center that a vehicle (Hywa) parked without driver got breaks free and slided backside which led to its collision with a Vehicle (Hywa) parked behind.
Getting information Shift I/c along with Qrt promptly acted and reached the spot. Ensured no severe injury has taken place and only minor injuries to both the vehicle drivers, they were sent to Hundru OHC. On investigeting the matter with eye witnesses at spot and CCTV footage, it was found that the driver - Anand Mahato (JH10BM0314) got down from vehicle as he was unable to move the vehicle ahead so he got down to apply the wheel chock howver by the time he could've react as the vehicle was on a slope started sliding behind and hit the parked vehicle (JH10AM9728) 50mtrs behind. Since, both vehicle runs under SUPL, no conflict was there related to damage. Alcohol check was carried out in which driver came clean.

INCIDENTTITLE: IR - Worker/ Union/ Transporters at CCP site at 25 Feb 2025, 8:00 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
A peaceful Dharna started by M/s PAPL Labours outside the Temple gate, starting from 25th February 2025, 8:00 Hrs. and continuing till 1st March 2025, 18:00 Hrs. The IR team and security team collaborated effectively to handle the situation, ensuring the safety and security of all personnel and property.

Incident Details:

- The Dharna was peaceful, with no reports of violence or damage to property.
- The IR team engaged with the PAPL labour union representatives to understand their grievances and work towards a resolution.
- The security team ensured that the Dharna did not disrupt the normal functioning of the plant and that all personnel and property were secure.
- The situation was closely monitored, and regular updates were provided to management.

Action Taken:

- The IR team and security team worked together to ensure a peaceful resolution to the Dharna.
- Regular communication was maintained with the PAPL labor union representatives to address their concerns.
- The situation was closely monitored, and contingency plans were in place to ensure business continuity.

Conclusion:

The peaceful Dharna was handled effectively by the IR team and security team which was done outside the company premise, ensuring the safety and security of all personnel and property. The situation was resolved without any disruptions to the business.

INCIDENTTITLE: Suspicious activity of theft
INCIDENTDETAILS:
On 4th March 2025, at around 0310hrs, Civil Security Guard Arvind Kumar posted at main gate noticed

that an unidentified person suspiciously approached near to the copper ingot truck bearing Reg No

KA567350 which was parked outside of the main gate. Main gate Security Guard Arvind Kumar noticed

the movement of a miscreant at the truck and immediately rushed towards the parked truck. Observing

the security person approaching unidentified person ran away. Security supervisor ExSM Satendra Singh

conducted search operation in the area along with Arvindra Kumar, but no material or suspicious persons

were found. Satendra Kumar also checked the vehicle and material for any ingot theft. But material was

found intact. Further Satendra Kuma informed to Security SIC Madhan Kumar and alerted other truck

drivers present in the area. SIC Madhan Kumar further investigated and reported about the incident to

CSO and Head Logistics.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at IRON ORE KARNATAKA site at 2024-08-08 02:00
INCIDENTDETAILS:
At above Mentioned details, Mr. Siraj, Mr. Dadapeer, Mr. Imran, Mr. Shriram and some Chitradurga Adiru Mattu Saraku Samagrigala Saganike Lorry Malikara Sangha peoples came to JSW Y junction in Bolero vehicle. On duty Security Guard enquired and they confirmed that they were going to visit JSW Mines related to their vehicle issue. Since JSW Y junction is the common junction for Vedanta mines and JSW Mines. Security Guard opened the Manual barrier and they parked the bolero vehicle inbetween the barrier closing place so that security guard did not closed the barrier. 
Now they demanded the key of the Vedanta gate available at Y Junction to come towards the Gate 2. Security staff denied for the key. They get down from vehicle, pushed to security guard and broken the lock with the help of Metal rod.
Meanwhile they called to civil trucks parked near to the barrier and forcefully taken entry inside from JSW Y Junction towards gate 2. They parked 09 nos of trucks approx. 100mtr inside the gate to block the complete road and after sometimes they returned back.

Available security staff informed to on duty ASO and other concerned persons. Immediately matter informed to Police Help line number and Rural Police Station. Additional Security Manpower, Bouncers and Security officer diverted towards the location. 
At around 0400Hrs, 04 Police staff (02 from SP office and 02 Rural Police Station) reached at location and Security team briefed about the whole situation.

Police complaint was given to Rural Police station and copied to DySP,  SP , Chitradurga Deputy Conservator of Forest, and sought for proper and necessary protection.

After discussion All the civil trucks were removed from the location on 26.08.2024 at around 0200 hrs

INCIDENTTITLE: Property Related at BALCO site at 2024-08-09 13:20
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 09.08.2024, at approximately 1320 hrs., Mr. Mukesh Singh, the on-duty guard deployed in the mill area, observed suspicious activity , He immediately reported it to the Zone In-Charge, Mr. Chandan Kumar Mishra. Acting promptly on the information, the security team was mobilized and strategically divided into two groups: one team approached the area from outside the premises, while the other advanced from within, converging on the mill area. Upon reaching the site, the security team discovered a group of 8-10 miscreants actively engaged in an attempt to steal copper cables by passing them through the boundary wall. The security team responded swiftly and efficiently, successfully recovering approximately 190 kg. of copper cable, value of Rs. 76000. The miscreants, upon noticing security movement, fled the premises empty-handed. The recovered copper cables have been securely stored in the security office.

The Security Guard stationed there, Mr. Raghvendra Prajapati, promptly informed the team of their presence.The Police team was also notified at Emergency Helpline Number 112 & arrived at the scene. The miscreants fled the location empty-handed upon the police's arrival. During the incident, Mr. Ram Rahis Patel, stationed at the mill area, sustained a minor chest injury as a result of the stone pelting and was taken to the hospital for treatment by Mr. Chandan Mishra. After receiving treatment, Mr. Ram Rahis Patel returned home.

INCIDENTTITLE: Theft of asset at SILVASSA site at 09 Jun 2024, 12:00 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
On 10th June 2024, IT Engineer Brijesh Singh reported at Security office at around 1130hrs, raising suspicion about a missing computer mouse, out of 4, which was kept at a desk in the commercial office. Control room operator Bhim Sharma checked the CCTV footages from the time the PCs were kept at the office, and found that on 8th June 2024, in C-shift, Admin helper Jitesh Kanoja while collecting housekeeping material from office areas, had discretely grabbed one mouse from the desk in commercial office, and taken it to his home. Security team summoned Jitesh to the office and questioned him regarding the theft, to which he accepted his deeds. 

He also accepted having taken extra ten packets of biscuits (snacks for night shift business partners)  after the night shift on 9th June 2024, while he is only allowed to take two.

The stolen computer mouse was kept in Jitesh’s home, which he was asked to return and submit at office. He submitted the stole computer mouse to the office on 12th June 2024.

INCIDENTTITLE: Access Related Violations at BALCO site at 2024-06-16 06:40
INCIDENTDETAILS: On June 16th, 2024, at approximately 0640 hrs, Mr. Bharathidasan (UID 1599377) and Mr. Dushyant Kumar Shah (UID 1605597), business partners of M/s NTPC GE Power Service Private, were apprehended for proxy punching while attempting to exit the premises. SG Bhupendra, stationed at the Parsabhata Pedestrian gate, caught Mr. Dushyant Kumar Shah in the act and promptly notified the authorities. Mr. Dushyant Kumar Shah attempted to exit during the C shift. Having lost his gate pass, Mr. Dushyant Kumar Shah asked Mr. Bharathidasan to provide his gate pass for exiting the premises. Instead of informing the security team, Mr. Bharathidasan provided his gate pass to Mr. Dushyant Kumar Shah for exiting the plant. The Security Supervisor, Mr. RB Chandrakar, conducted a subsequent investigation which revealed that Mr. Bharathidasan facilitated this unauthorized exit.

SG Sudama Das Manikpuri, stationed at the Parsabhata Pedestrian gate, caught Mr. Abhishek Kumar Tiwari in the act and promptly notified the authorities. Mr. Mithlesh Kumar was in G shift. Instead of retrieving his gate pass, which was left in the room, Mr. Abhishek Kumar Tiwari , provided his gate pass to Mr. Mithalesh Kumar for exiting the plant. The Security Supervisor, Mr. R B Chandrakar, conducted a subsequent investigation which revealed that Mr. Abhishek Kumar Tiwari facilitated this unauthorized exit.

After questioning Mr. Dalai (UID:-1597786) claimed he was taking the materials outside the plant for maintenance, but his actions appeared suspicious. Consequently, Supervisor Sanjay Singh confiscated Mr. Dalai's gate pass and seized the materials. INCIDENTTITLE: Damage to Property at SILVASSA site at 11 Jun 2024, 3:30 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
On 07th June 2024, at around 0540hrs. Rathod Transport vehicle no DN09N9106 reported at new parking yard, Traffic Marshal Aarif returned the vehicle as parking was full, while reversing the vehicle, it hit the roof of house which is situated opposite to New Parking Yard Gate and poultry cage of house owner got damaged. Transporter Supervisor Mr Pandey reached the incident location discussed with house owner Mr Salman and agreed to pay compensation Security SIC Yeeshu Chauhan reached at incident spot cleared the traffic and reported the incident to Head Security.
On 09 June 2024, at around 1415hrs. Mr. Salman warned Traffic Mashall Rahul to stop the vehicle movement at the new parking yard, as the damage had not been settled till then. Security SIC Yeeshu Chauhan along with transporter supervisor Mr Yadav reached at new parking yard and Transporter Supervisor connected call between Mr Salman and Rathod Transport Manager Mr Dubey. The talks ended positively.
On 11th June 2024, at around 0330hrs. 4 people (Identified drunken) broke the main glass of Raman Transport vehicle MP09HH4062 which was standing near substation corner tea stall, outside the main plant area. After that they entered the new parking yard and tried to break the Grievance box. After receiving a call from New Parking Yard Traffic Marshall, Security SIC Deepak Gangoda immediately sent QRT. QRT reached at the new parking yard and all the 4 people dispersed.

INCIDENTTITLE: Access Control at RDC site at 19 Jun 2024, 8:28 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
It is to bring to your attention that, on 19th June 2024 at 20:28hrs, two persons Mr. Om Prakash Regar(consultant gate pass C-157) of M/s Vaaman and Mr. Mahendra Jat( permanent gate pass SKM0025003) of M/s Vaaman were caught doing Proxy Punch at Punch IN turnstile.

Mahendra entered through turnstile when Om Prakash punched IN with his card. He was stopped by the security Guard and was asked to Punch Out and leave. Meanwhile, Om Prakash went inside the premises. When he was leaving, we took his statement which is attached.

He states that Proxy punching was done by mistake. According to our investigation of the incident we found out that Mahendra went outside to Ghati Area (using proxy punch) and later tried getting inside on Om Prakash's consultant card. We find both guilty of proxy punching

At around 0145 hrs QRT 1 informed Security Control room that 02 vehicles had collided with each other in the dry coal yard. On investigation it was found that truck No WB23E 9165 of Amrit freight carrier which had brought BF coke from Jindal was already parked there. Hywa No JH09AP 6698 of JRL mining  which had brought BF coke from Bandhdih siding collided with the left side cabin of the truck No WB23E 9165 while reversing for unloading the vehicle due to which the left side looking glass and door handle got damaged.

Traffic team imposed penalty of Rs-1000/- for unsafe driving. Information has been passed to Mr Sudipt Sah Shift I/C of Coke Oven and JRL supervisor Mr Krishna Jha for sorting out the matter. INCIDENTTITLE: Theft of asset in commercial Office  at SILVASSA site at 08 Jun 2024, 12:06 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
On 10th June 2024, IT Engineer Brijesh Singh reported at Security office at around 1130hrs, raising suspicion about a missing computer mouse, out of 4, which was kept at a desk in the commercial office. Control room operator Bhim Sharma checked the CCTV footages from the time the PCs were kept at the office, and found that on 8th June 2024, in C-shift, Admin helper Jitesh Kanoja while collecting housekeeping material from office areas, had discretely grabbed one mouse from the desk in commercial office, and taken it to his home. Security team summoned Jitesh to the office and questioned him regarding the theft, to which he accepted his deeds. 

He also accepted having taken extra ten packets of biscuits (snacks for night shift business partners)  after the night shift on 9th June 2024, while he is only allowed to take two.

The stolen computer mouse was kept in Jitesh’s home, which he was asked to return and submit at office. He submitted the stole computer mouse to the office on 12th June 2024.

INCIDENTTITLE: Access Related Violations at BALCO site at 2024-06-17 09:00
INCIDENTDETAILS:
On June 17, 2024, while validating Daily Access Data records against CCTV footage, an incident involving multiple In-Punches was flagged. The Security Operations Centre (SOC) discovered that contractor worker Mr. Ajay Nikam (UID 1607216) from M/s Larsen Turbo Limited facilitated the unauthorized entry of his co-worker Mr. Rampravesh Rao (UID 1605562) into the plant premises on June 16, 2022, during the G shift at around 0929 hrs from the project gate.

During the analysis, it was found that Mr. Rampravesh Rao's gate pass was not working. Mr. Ajay Nikam allowed him to enter using his gate pass and subsequently entered using his own pass. Mr. Ajay Nikam was called in for an investigation. During the interrogation, he admitted to allowing Mr. Rampravesh Rao entry because his gate pass was not working, and Mr. Rao was already late for an important task.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at IRON ORE KARNATAKA site at 2024-05-19 19:15

On dated 19.05.2024 at around 20:00Hrs, received message from the Safety department about the theft of material from the safety storeroom (Safety Shoe and Jacket). 

Surveillance increased in the particular area to track the suspicious movement and again on dated 25.05.2024 theft attempt made in safety storeroom for the theft of Safety shoes and safety jacket. 

Based on available CCTV Footage and punch records following person were identified as involved in the theft attempt.  

Sandeepa S working under Chaitanya Geochem & Kamplesh .H & Babu.O working under Saliz.CO 

Detailed investigation was carried out and all persons accepted their mistake, and they were taking out the material from the window of store room. Latter their cards were blocked and lodged Police complaint. 
List of Material missing –  

Fire pump battery-2, Rope- 2 roll, Pipe- 8 rolls, Bags- 8, Gift box -2, Shoes- 6 pairs, Torch- 3 

Pen- 100, Jacket- 10 

Approx cost of theft material – 40,000/-

INCIDENTTITLE: Theft of Copper at SILVASSA site at 26 Jun 2024, 4:15 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 26th June 2024, around 1615hrs SG Gulshed Khan posted at Parking Yard Gate observed suspicious behavior from SK Electrical Helper Vinod Laxi Gavli during the gate out. He immediately called him for security checks, but Vinod Laxi Gavli turned back and went to the BP changing room. SG Gulshed Khan quickly informed the security control room and Head Security. Head Security reached at BP changing room and checks were done in the lobby of Vinod Laxi Gavli, upon asking he accepted theft attempt of Copper Armored pieces which he was to sell at Scrap Shop located near Rakholi Petrol Pump. The security team confiscated around 9kg of copper armored from him and the incident has been immediately informed to the Manager Growth Projects. Supervisor HK Electrical Vikrant Dhangar was called on, and Vinod Laxi Gavli submitted an apology letter.

INCIDENTTITLE: Material at VAL J site at 15 Jul 2024, 5:10 PM

"At about 1710 hours, Ash team from Ash siding area reported that 5-6 miscreants have entered the plant by breaching the boundary wall near Bhurkhamunda bridge area. The Baaz team immediately reached the spot and after seeing the security team, the miscreants ran away leaving the iron rods and pipes. The surrounding areas were thoroughly searched and nothing was found except iron rods and pieces of pipes. 
However, it was found that the height of the boundary from outside is too low, about 6-7 feet, which could be crossed very easily and two pieces of bamboo were deployed inside the boundary wall to get support for climbing down from the boundary. The bamboo support was removed, and the recovered material was handed over to the ash team and alerted to inform security if any suspicious activity was noticed."

INCIDENTTITLE: Access Related Violations at BALCO site at 2024-06-19 20:40
INCIDENTDETAILS: On June 19th 2024, approximately 2040 hrs., Mr. Kamlesh Kumar Karsh (UID 1599226) and Mr. Punaram Lasar (UID 1596173), business partners of M/s KEC International Limited, were apprehended for proxy punching to exit the premises. SG Ramasish Chauhan, stationed at the Parsabhata gate Incharge, caught Mr. Kamlesh Kumar Karsh in the act and promptly notified the authorities. Mr. Kamlesh Kumar Karsh attempted to exit during the G shift. Instead of retrieving his gate pass, which was left in the room, Mr. Punaram Lasar provided his gate pass to Mr. Kamlesh Kumar Karsh for exiting the plant. The Security Incharge , Mr. Prashant Yadav, conducted a subsequent investigation which revealed that Mr. Punaram Lasar facilitated this unauthorized exit.

INCIDENTTITLE: Access Related Violations at BALCO site at 2024-06-27 18:03
INCIDENTDETAILS: On June 27th, 2024, at approximately 1803 hrs., Mr. Rajendra Kumar (UID 1634511), a business partner of M/s Larsen & Toubro Limited, was caught in the act of tailgating while attempting to exit the plant premises. SG Brij Kishore Singh, stationed at the Alumina Gate, observed the incident, and promptly notified the authorities. Mr. Rajendra Kumar was in G shift and had forgotten to carry his gate pass. Instead of retrieving his gate pass, Mr. Rajendra Kumar attempted to exit with another worker by tailgating. The Security Shift In-Charge of Plant 1 conducted a subsequent investigation, revealing that Mr. Rajendra Kumar committed this tailgating violation..

INCIDENTTITLE: Access Related Violations at BALCO site at 2024-06-28 19:30
INCIDENTDETAILS: On June 28th 2024, at approximately 1930 hrs., Mr. Arun Prasad (UID 1608659) and Mr. Ajay Kumar (UID 1605298), business partners of M/s PRAJ Industries Limited, were caught in the act of Tailgating while attempting to exit the plant premises. SG Sudama Das Manikpuri, stationed at the Parsabhata Pedestrian Gate, caught Mr. Arun Prasad in the act and promptly notified the authorities. Mr. Arun Prasad did not have his own gate pass. Instead of retrieving his gate pass, the gate pass of Mr. Ajay Kumar attempted to exit during the G shift. The Security Zone incharge of Plant 3, conducted a subsequent investigation which revealed that Mr. Arun Prasad doing this unauthorized exit.

INCIDENTTITLE: Agitation by community and workers at SESA COKE - GUJARAT site at 02 Jul 2024, 1:41 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 01-07-2024 at 12:40 pm, Labor of M/s RBM parked the bike no. GJ12DH1260 of a person named Shri Varchand Rohin village Lunwa .  Outside Main Gate Unit-2. At around 12:40 in the night, two miscreants came and stole his bike because his bike was not locked. The security team helped in registering the FIR and also examined the CCTV footage. On 02-07-2024 at 10:00 am, Unit-2 Dispatch staff came to Unit-1 gate and agitated by demanding bike parking facility inside the plant or deployment of security guards outside the parking area. Due to which dispatch work remained stopped for 06 hours. The security team, CHRO, head operation discussed with him and tried to convince him. Also assured to provide vehicles for shifting from village to plant. At 18:00 the matter was resolved, and all dispatch workers went to work.

INCIDENTTITLE: Fire or Explosion at FPL site at 2024-07-05 14:59
INCIDENTDETAILS:
On June 5, 2024, at approximately 14:59 hrs, we received information from Mr. Soubhagya Panda regarding an unusual sound coming from the switchyard. We promptly alerted the ambulance, QRT team, safety officer, and security officer. Upon reaching the site, they discovered that one transformer had caught fire. 
The safety officers quickly extinguished the fire, and fortunately, there were no injuries or medical emergencies. By 16:01, the situation had returned to normal with no causalities.
LNAME: Power Plant, Boiler, HSD Tank area, Crusher house, Wagon tippler, TG building, Weigh bridge & Coal yard

INCIDENTTITLE: Theft/Pilferage at MIDSTREAM (PIPELINE) site at 01 Feb 2025, 5:30 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
During a routine frisking procedure, Mr. Chavda Dhruv Kumar  Sopan employee was found to be carrying a joint coupling within their tiffin box. The employee did not have prior approval or authorization to possess this item.
This incident raises concerns about workplace security protocols. Further investigation is warranted to determine the employee’s intent.
Based on the following incident, a detailed investigation was carried out jointly by the Security team along with the Sopan Site Supervisor.

No prior approval or authorization was evident for Dhruv to carry the material outside the company premises.

Hence, this is considered a ZTB, and the required procedural action has been taken by the Security team. He will be blocked from further access to all Cairn-Vedanta locations.

Considering this incident, you are requested to take appropriate measures to raise awareness among the site team and provide a confirmation of compliance.

INCIDENTTITLE: Water tankers drivers stopped the water tanker movements at IRON ORE KARNATAKA site at 05 Feb 2025, 12:00 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
"Date: 05 February 2025.
Time: 12:00 to 1300 hrs.
Water tanker movement stopped by water tanker drivers.
Location: Water tanker parking yard.
Summary:
On 05 February 2025 at approximately 12:00 hrs., a group of water tanker drivers started a water tanker stop at the water tanker parking yard.
At approximately 09:00 hrs. at North Block, the drivers stopped the movement of water tankers to seek an apology from the Manjunath Contractor Supervisor Mr. Chetan for his alleged misbehavior with Mr. Adarsh ??(driver of water tanker #4231).
Following this incident, the water tanker drivers collectively decided to stop the movement of water tankers and demanded an apology from Mr. Chetan.
Action taken:
The operation team was informed immediately after receiving the information. A Quick Response Team (QRT) was dispatched to the water tanker parking yard and engaged in discussions with the drivers to assess the situation.
The operation team (Mr. Somashekhar Reddy) engaged in discussions with the water tanker drivers. After this intervention, the work stoppage was resolved and water tanker movement resumed at approximately 13:00 hrs."

INCIDENTTITLE: Theft Attempt foiled | ZSD site
INCIDENTDETAILS: ZSD Security Team executed a significant operation basis the intelligence inputs, this morning, June 28, 2024, at approximately 0915 hrs leading to apprehension of Two perpetrator’s. During the operation, two Kalbeliya women were apprehended while attempting theft in the Colony area. Early this morning, they entered through the boundary area and attempted to break into a B-type house near the Mandir. the security teams continuous patrolling with the QRT, detected the suspicious movement. Immediate liaison was established with the Pratap Nagar Police team, and a formal complaint has been lodged following the coordination with CI Pratap Nagar. The suspects are now in police custody, and further investigations are underway to determine their involvement in other thefts in the area. The security team is also liaising with the internal team to immediately close the open patch from where the intrusion occurred. The apprehended individuals are identified as - 1) Poonam, W/O Bhagwati, resident of Meetha Neem, aged 23, and 2) Meera, D/O Amar Kalbeliya, resident of Meetha Neem, aged 19. Both suspects attempted to access the colony were caught red-handed in possession of stolen items worth 7500/- approx .

INCIDENTTITLE: Dharna at BALCO site at 02 Oct 2024, 7:53 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 02/10/2024, at around 0753 hrs., around 15-20 women from the Unemployed Women’s Committee arrived at the BALCO Project Gate with banners and blocked the entrance as part of their protest regarding employment in Balco plant. The security team, already stationed at the gate based on prior information received through the committee's letter, and monitoring the situation. The matter was immediately communicated to all concerned stakeholders, including the police. The police, along with a lady constable, arrived at the scene to assess the situation. Following discussions with the external team and representatives from the committee, the protestors decided to end the blockade, and the gate was reopened smoothly at 1410 hrs., The coordination and prompt response of the security team, combined with the police presence, ensured that the situation remained peaceful and under control throughout.

INCIDENTTITLE: Any Other at IRON ORE ORISSA site at 06 Nov 2024, 9:45 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
There was a incident at IOO Feegrade mine yesterday 5th Nov’24  at around 9.45 hrs to  22.00 hrs involving a outbound truck OD29E4930  deployed by transporter Fortune Logistics and destined for Paradeep Port.    Prima Facie the  trucks loaded with Iron ore fines  seems to have lost control due to brake failure on the slope and dashed against the iron ore fines stack.   

The driver Sukumar Mallick aged around 43 years got head injuries and other bruises on his body .   He was shifted to Koenjhar Govt hospital  by our ALS ambulance after the initial treatment at the dispensary at the mines.   He was admitted in the hospital and received some stitches on the head.    

The truck is also damaged due to the impact of the crash 

   As per the Transporter’s representative, the IP is stable  as per the doctors .   The senior doctors will check him again and recommend the further course of action.    I spoke to the IP and he conveyed that he had pain at the injury area and was feeling weak .   His family members have also reached the hospital today morning.
LNAME: Top of the North Pit areas

INCIDENTTITLE: PR - Political/ Social at IRON ORE KARNATAKA site at 2024-11-25 16:50
INCIDENTDETAILS:
On dated 25th Nov 2024 at around 1650Hrs, Forest Range officer (RFO) Mr. Satish along with o6 nos of forest officer came to admin building. 
Initially they carried out physical land survey near to diesel bunk. They brought one JCB from outside and started making trench from West to East direction inside admin building covering Workshop washing point, Security office, Diesel bunk.

Available officer at site talked to RFO and he clearly told that this land is part of Survey no. 20 and ownership is with forest department. Vedanta team made the illegal encroachment and same to be cleared.

At around 1900Hrs, RFO returned back from the site and other forest officer available at site to continue the trench job.

Forest team continue the trench work up to TSD Crusher plant. They also made vertical trench near to diesel bunk, one parallel trench covering admin Vehicle & Pedestrian lane, and trench outside workshop perimeter to avoid any vehicle movement from workshop opening area.

At around 2115Hrs, Forest team along with JCB returned.

INCIDENTTITLE: Intrusion at tamira#1 township TUTICORIN site at 07 Dec 2024, 1:30 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS:
On 07th Dec 2024 at 01:30 hrs. Security guard (Mr. Muniasamy) of Tamira#1 canteen backside duty post observed stones falling sounds from perimeter. When he rushed to the spot, He observed two-person scaled over the perimeter wall and about to jump inside. Then he shouted at them and tossed stones at the miscreants. They shouted back at him not to throw stones and prayed to go away. Then both jumped outside from the wall. Security Guard Mr. Munia Samy gave signal to OS parking guard (Mr. Manikandan V) through long whistles. Then OS parking Guard also rushed to the spot from the outside of perimeter wall. Two miscreants who attempted intrusion fled from the scene. 

Mr. G Mallesham, HoD security, Mr. Subburaj, TPP SO and Mr. Ramamurthy, Copper SO rushed to the spot and further investigation done. Assessed possible entry and exit points. The perimeter found gaps at some places, height was low and no overhead concertina coil.

2. Action taken: 
1.	The Security Team promptly acted upon the incident and foiled the intrusion attempt.
2.	Entire security shift personnel have been alerted and briefed.

3. Recommendations:
3.	Perimeter wall to be strengthened.
4.	Overhead Concertina coil to be placed.
5.	Damaged wall repairing to be done on war foot.
6.	Canal water passage rusted pipes to change to grill or rods and extra gap to be closed.
7.	Scrap lying near the perimeter wall to be removed.

INCIDENTTITLE: Dharna at IRON ORE KARNATAKA site at 2024-12-08 07:40
INCIDENTDETAILS:
02 Civil trucks came from JSW mines, on reaching at JSW Y junction they taken right turn and blocked the Gate 2 movement at JSW Y Junction. Mr. Siraj, Anish, Imran, Irfan, Shriram, Shivana, came to the location and sit on road by lay down the trapline for the various demands like Start of Gate 3 movement, dust compensation to farmers having land adjacent to Rly Track, illegal mining post 1800Hrs.
Matter informed to Police helpline number and Mr. Kiran went to Rural Police station for lodging the Police Complaint.
Siraj Kept Photographs of Dr. Bhimrao Ambedkar with garland and started slogans against Vedanta mines.
At around 0930Hrs, One Police constable came to site from Hireguntur Police Station.
Gradually numbers of protestor increased, and count became more than 30 and maximum people from Bheemsamundra village and Hire Kandwadi village.
Media persons from New one Channel reached at site for the coverage of this activity and during media coverage they raised slogans against Vedanta.
Meanwhile additional Security manpower, Bouncer and Workmen diverted towards the location.
At around 1050Hrs, one external Vehicle reached at site with Tent and chairs to install at location however available Vedanta team did not allow them and heated argument happened from both sides.
They also installed one poster at truck against the mines.

Meanwhile Mr. Kiran (Security) and Mr. Ravi Nayaka (ER/PR) submitted the written Police Complaint at Rural Police Station and Dy SP office.

At different time intervals different team reached at location, 1250Hrs Circle Inspector from Rural Police Station and its team, 1300Hrs Mr. Ravi Basaguppi and Mr. Shrinivas Pattar, 1305Hrs Mr. Ramesh Dist. President and Mr. Vijay Kumar Dist. Vice President from Kannada Rakshana Vedika, 1440Hrs DMG officer Mr. Madusudan, 1500Hrs, Dy SP along with reserve police. 
Discussion started in presence of DMG, Police, revenue officer and Vedanta team and following were their demands – Start of Gate 3 movement, Illegal dumping, employment to local peoples, Admin gate sealed by Tehsildar and Vedanta team is using the same and they submitted one letter to revenue officer.

At around 1820Hrs, Siraj, Anees, Sriram, Dy SP, Police Inspector left for meeting in Chitradurga.
At around 2225Hrs, All the persons protesting at site return back from the location.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at SILVASSA site at 2024-10-05 05:50
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 5th October 2024, at around 0610hrs Security SIC Yeeshu Chauhan received a call from CCPC SIC Bhagavath Kumar to stop the CCPC Furnace Helper Anil Kumar Singh going out from the plant and upon asking the reason to stop he asked Security SIC to immediately come in CCPC Office. SIC Yeeshu Chauhan immediately instructed to main gate security guards to check ID cards of all the outgoing workers and restrict Anil Kumar Singh from CCPC to gate out. Security SIC immediately reached to the CCPC Office and CCPC SIC explained that he has observed Anil Kumar walking in suspicious condition with 2 blister lug pieces wrapped in plastic using transparent tape. Security SIC confiscated the material and instructed to main gate SGs for stopping shift out, he reached at gate and checked the gate passes of workers, Anil Kumar Singh detained for interrogation and rest of the shift was released. Head Security reached to the plant for interrogation in which Anil Kumar Singh accepted his intentions to steal copper blister pieces. As per apology letter submitted by Anil Kumar and the security investigation findings during the C shift exit in early morning on 05th October 2024, Anil Kumar took 2 blister lug pieces from CCPC charging area and wrapped them in plastic after that he putted them near to CCPC BPs locker from where he was to take material out in his bag, CCPC SIC was also coming at the same time from CCPC washroom and he caught Anil Kumar in suspicion with blister lug pieces. Bhagavath Kumar immediately taken the copper pieces from Anil also informed the same to CCPC furnace shift associate S Prashant and Security SIC.

INCIDENTTITLE: Select at RDC site at 04 Oct 2024, 4:21 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On October 4, 2024, at HZAPL, around 18:50 hrs, a loaded zinc cathode vehicle made contact with an electrical wire near Sindesar Kalan village, resulting in the wire breaking and hanging down. The villagers stopped the vehicle for 1 hour and 20 minutes. Around 21:00 hrs, four villagers from Sindesar Kalan arrived at the HZAPL gate by car and inquired about the exit of the zinc cathode vehicle from the plant. All four villagers were intoxicated and misbehaved with the security supervisor. Some of them forcefully entered the plant, seized the key to the cathode vehicle, and left.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at BALCO site at 2025-01-12 19:23
INCIDENTDETAILS: On January 12, 2025, at around 1923hrs, security guard Mr. Jaiman Munda noticed suspicious behavior from Hyva driver Mr. Aman Singh (ID No: 1607274) during routine frisking at the Alumina Gate. Mr. Singh, Business Partner by M/S L&T and subcontractor Vivek Construction, had unusual bulges under his clothing. A thorough check revealed 7 kg of copper wire worth approximately Rs. 2800 concealed on his body. Mr. Singh admitted to the theft during interrogation. His statement was recorded, his gate pass was blocked, and the matter was escalated to the HR department for further action.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at BALCO site at 2025-01-13 13:50
INCIDENTDETAILS: On January 13, 2025, at around 1350hrs, we received critical information by Security Mitra about a vehicle (CG12BH6518) allegedly involved in copper wire theft. This information was relayed to Plant-3 in-charge, Mrs. Pushpanjali Chouhan, and the QRT Team. The security team located the vehicle at the Parsabhata 2W out gate. Upon investigation, Mr. Ajay Kumar Yadav (UID 1594233) from M/s NTPC GE Power Services Private (subcontracted by M/s Feedback Power O&M Services Pvt Ltd, under subcontract by M/s Mandyanti Enterprises) was found involved. A frisking revealed 2.7 kg of copper wire concealed on his body and in his shoes. The suspects were apprehended, and their statements were recorded. The suspect was handed over to the police, and police complaint was lodged, and his gate pass was blocked.

INCIDENTTITLE: Unauthorized entry/ Intrusion/Trespassing at VAL J site at 30 Jan 2025, 12:12 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: At approximately 1215 hrs in the (V-18) near Bhagipalli railway gate area, five villagers were observed engaging in suspicious movement, attempting to intrude into the plant through a gap in the newly constructed boundary wall with the intention to steal property. The QRT and BAAZ Team were immediately informed. Upon receiving the information, both teams reached the spot. Realizing the swift action of the QRT and BAAZ Team, the villagers fled the scene. The situation was normalized by 1235 hrs

INCIDENTTITLE: Terrorist /Naxal strikes at SESA COKE - GUJARAT site at 21 Jan 2025, 6:00 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 21.01.2025 at around 1500 hrs , RBM workers are asking for final payment settlement as per commitment of Ms RBM. But payment is not done. Workers are asking for payment clarity and CL, PL payment clarity. Our operation and commercial team was discussing the issue. At 1800 our employees bus was stopped by RBM workers for asking clarity from Vedanta side. Security team and RBM project manager discussed the issue with local and the bus was released at 1835 hrs. About 50 workers are outside the gate peacefully. Exco team discussing the issue and at plant. In between some drunken workers threw a couple of stone at gate. It was decided that we will verify the leaves by today 1100 hrs after discussion with HO team, Praveen George and Karni. We discussed the same with workers. All were removed peacefully.

INCIDENTTITLE: SOP- LSR Violation at VAL J site at 02 Feb 2025, 4:10 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: At around 0410hrs, a Loaded Coal Truck (OD23H7565) from Kalyani Transport was unloaded at CPP Hopper Phase-1 without undergoing the mandatory sampling process. Upon interviewing the driver, it was found that he had requested a sampling stamp twice at the Auger-B Sampling Point, but the operator, Padmanath Omath (MMSPL), denied his request and instructed him to proceed for unloading. At the unloading point, Supervisor Rohit Singh (Chennai Radha) also failed to check for the sampling stamp and provided the unloading stamp. The discrepancy was identified by Kartik Shetty at the Challan Office when the driver came for the final out stamp. On preliminary investigation It was revealed that the truck had been unloaded without sampling. The vehicle is currently on hold for further investigation, and all relevant documents have been confiscated by the security team and coal team has been informed about this.

INCIDENTTITLE: ASSET/PROPERTY at VZI site at 03 Jan 2025, 8:10 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: Theft of 11 cordless drills which were stored inside an office at the Deeps Offices. there was no forced entry and drills just disappeared. no precise date or time can be given as to when such could've happened.

INCIDENTTITLE: PR - Villagers/ Neighborhood at SUVALI (OFFSHORE) site at 12 Feb 2025, 10:30 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 12.02.2025 at 1030 hrs, some construction activities have started by Hazira Notified area Authorities our side of Suvali terminal ( west side). Security team stopped the excavation activities as it was near Cairn ROU pipelines. SVL IM, OPS & LAQ team informed to visit the site and take this matter with concern authorities & take appropriate action to protect the Pipeline.

INCIDENTTITLE: Dharna at RAVVA (OFFSHORE) site at 03 Jan 2025, 12:00 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: Dharna by unemployed youth union members asking for job opportunities by the company. Started at 1200 hrs and with peaceful discussion ended by 1400 hrs.

INCIDENTTITLE: IR - Worker/ Union/ Transporters at RAVVA (OFFSHORE) site at 13 Jan 2025, 7:45 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: Workers gathered at main gate asking the management to fast forward the process of paying of annual bonus to the workers.

INCIDENTTITLE: Fire or Explosion at CCP site at 26 Jan 2025, 7:39 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: At Around 1939 Hrs. received information that there is fire incident at 45 MVA furnace floor 3. QRT along with Ambulance reached at site. QRT team extinguished the fire and there is no injury reported.

INCIDENTTITLE: PR - Villagers/ Neighborhood at SUVALI (OFFSHORE) site at 18 Feb 2025, 9:50 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 18 Feb 2025 at 0950 hrs; Stakeholders from Suvali Village came to the Suvali Terminal Main Gate and asked to take back the VTS Operator who was asked to leave following disciplinary action. Head Security Offshore along with Admin Manager tried to counsel them but they have further escalated this matter to the local political stakeholder. Again at 1230 hrs, a meeting was held at terminal office with the Stakeholders including village Sarpanch and reinforced that there is no justification or leniency for disciplinary cases. On further discussion reached on mutual agreement to consider local replacements for backfilling the referred positions.

INCIDENTTITLE: Access Control at VAB site at 01 Nov 2024, 11:06 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: At 11:06 hrs, Shambhu Phadte, a resident of Amona, blocked the PID Security main gate by stopping his vehicle. He claimed Monomark, the new vendor, had stopped his vehicle from operating. Despite attempts by security to resolve the issue, Mr. Phadte refused to move, causing a disruption. Police were contacted, convinced him to move, and he was sent for further discussion with Mr. Prasad Ajgaonkar. The gate was cleared, and normal operations resumed.

INCIDENTTITLE: Material at VAB site at 17 Nov 2024, 3:26 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: A driver from Shivraj Gauns Transport attempted to unload pig iron without authorization. The Night Shift Dispatch In-Charge, Mr. Deepak, alerted security, but the driver escaped, claiming he needed water. He was apprehended the next morning, with the help of the tipper owner, and handed over to the police.

INCIDENTTITLE: PR - Villagers/ Neighborhood at SESA COKE- VAZARE site at 09 Mar 2025, 4:25 PM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 09.03.2025 at 1530 hours, Mr. Raoji Mahalasekar (Ex contractor employee who worked under MDV) and Mr. Pandurang Mahalasekar working under MDV as helper in operations created a ruckus at the gate, threatened security personnel by showing a knife (koyta), threw stones at them, and halted the operation of the main gate. We immediately informed the police, and at 1600 hours, the Dodamarga police arrived at the plant gate and managed the situation.

INCIDENTTITLE: Unauthorized entry/ Intrusion/Trespassing at VAB site at 07 Jan 2025, 5:30 AM
INCIDENTDETAILS: On 7th January 2025 at approximately 05:30 hrs, an unknown individual, who appeared to be a cleaner associated with a Heavy Motor Vehicle (HMV), was apprehended near the Battery 1 area of MCD. Upon discovery, the individual was detained and subjected to an initial investigation by the security team. It appears that he had likely gained entry through scaling the DNG Gate Boundary wall area.
